search_queries_used: site:entnet.org acute otitis externa itching modifying factors guideline
site:dha.gov.ae ear canal itching guideline UAE
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic otitis externa, eczema, fungal infection, contact reaction, or cerumen diagnosis
automatic eardrop, antibiotic, steroid, antifungal, procedure, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Ear-canal itching is nonspecific and requires direct examination and differential assessment.
No medicine, dose, procedure, treatment, referral, or urgency is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People with acute ear-canal itching; diabetes, immunocompromise, prior radiotherapy, dermatologic disease, devices, trauma, or non-intact tympanic membrane need individualized review.
setting_applicability: Primary, urgent, or ENT ear-canal assessment.
UAE_applicability: International guidance requires UAE antimicrobial, prescribing, device, procedure, infection-control, and referral adaptation.
recency_verification: The official AAO-HNSF 2014 acute otitis externa fact sheet and exact action statements were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The 2014 update remains the current AAO-HNSF acute otitis externa guideline identified; material recency uncertainty remains.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact acute-otitis-externa symptom section supports itch, pain, discharge, onset, associated features, relevant negatives, modifying factors, general appearance, vital signs, and ENT examination without diagnosing infection.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports swimmer’s-ear terminology, water exposure, ear-canal inflammation, itching, fullness, hearing change, external-ear pain or tenderness, diabetes, immunocompromise, and tympanic-membrane context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact key-action section supports clinician concern, entered plan, safety net, and follow-up only when documented; it does not prescribe drops, antibiotics, wick placement, or referral.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports distinguishing AOE from other otalgia or otorrhoea, pain assessment, clinician-selected topical management, administration discussion, and reassessment after non-response.

unresolved_source_gaps: Laterality, water or device exposure, trauma, skin disease, pain, discharge, hearing, canal and membrane findings, diabetes, immune status, diagnosis, culture, treatment, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
